subject: [RFC PATCH 32/45] mm: page_alloc: proactive high-water trigger for SPB slab shrink

2026-04-30.patch_summary: This patch introduces a proactive high-water trigger to the SPB slab shrinker, which fires when a tainted SPB's free pages fall below a certain threshold due to non-movable allocations. This allows for more frequent triggering of the shrinker, rather than waiting until the SPB is exhausted. The patch also removes a 100ms time-based throttle from the queue_spb_slab_shrink function, allowing the worker's runtime to act as the natural rate-limiter. The changes aim to improve the responsiveness and effectiveness of the slab shrinker in managing memory pressure.
